confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_391173e5-d88b-4ca2-95a3-50f1f568afae read back as archived

evidence: The Staccato Site needs correspondence from andrey@kokoev.name where any sender or receiver has domain staccato2011.com. Current sender-only admission is both underpowered and unsafe as a substitute. This exposed a broader design need: filtering must be first-class at the mailbox boundary and should be explainable before sync writes local state.
proposal: Design canonical mailbox selection/filtering as a composable predicate model. It should support participant predicates over from, sender, to, cc, bcc; folder/container predicates; time windows; thread/conversation predicates; subject/body metadata predicates where appropriate; attachment metadata predicates; include/exclude composition; and explicit unknown-field behavior. The same predicate should drive dry-run preview, adapter-level remote narrowing where possible, local admission after normalization, and recorded sync evidence.
payload.recommendation: Treat participant-domain filtering as the first required predicate, but implement it through the general filtering model. Require a dry-run report showing matched counts and exclusion reasons before a Site may activate mailbox sync.
payload.summary: The Staccato mailbox gap should be closed as a serious filtering capability, not merely by patching allowed_sender_domains into a sender-or-receiver special case. Client Sites need to declare what mail belongs to the Site by participant, folder, thread, subject, date, labels/categories, attachments, and possibly account-local provenance, while preserving previewability and auditability before materialization.
payload.title: Mailbox sync needs principled filtering, not one-off sender patch

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
